allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking is the core ally of complex-adaptive-systems because it supplies the lens for wholes, interrelationships, and patterns of change.
allies.source_quote: The fundamental essence of thinking in terms of Complex Adaptive Systems stems from systems thinking.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops make complex-adaptive-systems operational by showing how outputs return as inputs and reshape later behavior.
allies.source_quote: Systems are feedback-dominated, meaning outputs are continually routed back as inputs.

allies.rationale_text: Leverage-points help complex-adaptive-systems avoid micromanaging parts by focusing on the small changes that can alter adaptation and coordination.
allies.source_quote: CAS is especially useful when improving the whole depends on understanding where a small change can alter learning loops, coordination, or adaptation.

antagonists.rationale_text: Optionality can clash with complex-adaptive-systems when constant branching churn prevents stable learning loops from forming.
antagonists.source_quote: complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with optionality (optionality) when option churn undermines stable learning loops needed for system adaptation.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks complex-adaptive-systems by filtering out the disconfirming feedback the system needs to adapt and self-correct.
antagonists.source_quote: complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when model-defending behavior filters out disconfirming feedback needed for the system to adapt and self-correct.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with optionality when option churn undermines stable learning loops needed for system adaptation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: complex-adaptive-systems vs optionality: complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with optionality (optionality) when option churn undermines stable learning loops needed for system adaptation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with confirmation-bias when model-defending behavior filters out disconfirming feedback needed for the system to adapt and self-correct.
structured_tensions.source_quote: complex-adaptive-systems vs confirmation-bias: complex-adaptive-systems conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when model-defending behavior filters out disconfirming feedback needed for the system to adapt and self-correct.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on adaptive-system allies and two clean explicit tensions around branch churn and model-defending behavior.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology or simulation overlays beyond the bounded first-order allies and explicit source tensions.
open_questions: The source also gestures toward second-order and incentive-rich antagonists, but optionality and confirmation-bias were the cleanest bounded fits into the current curated surface.
